source_file: 4-5.FL.2 Anchor Chart - SMART Goals and Achieving Your Dreams.pdf
output_file: 4-5.FL.2 Anchor Chart - SMART Goals and Achieving Your Dreams.md

analysis.summary: This document provides instructions for teachers to create an anchor chart with students about SMART Goals as part of a grades 4-5 financial literacy lesson. The anchor chart serves as a teaching tool and reference throughout the unit to help students understand how to set and achieve meaningful goals.

entities.name: 4-5.FL.2 Lesson - How Can SMART Goals Help You Achieve Your Dreams?
entities.description: A financial literacy lesson for grades 4-5 focused on using SMART goals to achieve dreams

entities.name: SMART Goals
entities.description: Goal-setting framework with Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound criteria

entities.name: Anchor Chart
entities.description: Visual teaching tool created collaboratively with students to reinforce lesson concepts

entities.name: Specific
entities.description: SMART goal component asking 'What exactly do I want to accomplish?'

entities.name: Measurable
entities.description: SMART goal component asking 'How can I track my progress toward this goal?'

entities.name: Achievable
entities.description: SMART goal component asking 'Is this a goal I can accomplish?'

entities.name: Relevant
entities.description: SMART goal component asking 'Is this an important goal that will help my future?'

entities.name: Time-bound
entities.description: SMART goal component asking 'When do I want to achieve this goal by?'

entities.name: Grades 4-5 Financial Literacy
entities.description: Financial literacy curriculum designed for elementary students in grades 4 and 5

entities.name: SmartBoard
entities.description: Interactive whiteboard technology that can be used to display and complete the anchor chart template

entities.name: Anchor Chart Template
entities.description: Pre-designed template for students to complete anchor charts individually or in small groups

relationships.context: The anchor chart is created as part of the SMART Goals lesson

relationships.target: 4-5.FL.2 Lesson - How Can SMART Goals Help You Achieve Your Dreams?
relationships.context: The lesson teaches students about SMART Goals framework
Specific is the 'S' component of SMART Goals
Measurable is the 'M' component of SMART Goals
Achievable is the 'A' component of SMART Goals
Relevant is the 'R' component of SMART Goals
Time-bound is the 'T' component of SMART Goals
